name: Old Fashioned Apple Cider Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Apples (Use cooking apples like Macs or Granny Smith's. Core and peel)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the apples, 1 cup of apple cider and sugar in a 3-quart saucepan.
Cook over high heat until the mixture comes to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 8 minutes or until the apples are tender. Drain the apples, reserving the syrup.
2. Add enough additional apple cider to the syrup to make 1 1/3 cups. Return the syrup and apples to the saucepan.
3. In a small bowl, combine the cornstarch and water. Stir until well blended.
4. Stir the cornstarch mixture and cinnamon into the apple mixture. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in the butter.
5. Roll out the remaining pastry to an 11-inch circle. Fold the pastry gently into quarters and cut slits in the folds.
6. Gently unfold the pastry on top of the filling and trim the edge to 1/2 inch beyond the rim of the pie tin. Fold the top crust under the lower crust and form a ridge by fluting the edge of the pie.
7. Bake in an oven set to 400 degrees F for 40 to 45 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack until slightly warm before cutting and serving.
